notes: mistral-vibe variant: one turn runs, the session exits cleanly, then is relaunched via `vibe --resume <SESSION_ID>` in the SAME cwd. Resume rehydrates through resume_existing_session, which reopens the ORIGINAL session_<ts>_<shortid> directory and APPENDS to its messages.jsonl (session_logger.py:407, cli.py:208-218) — no new dir is minted. The adapter keys the daemon session off that directory basename (sessionIDFromPath), so the SAME session_id row reappears across both PID lifetimes — ONE session id, TWO PIDs. Birth templated from the codex sibling: v1_session_birth matches the proc-<PID> presession entering ready at launch, and because the real session is what goes working, the turn/exit/resume phases pin to v1_turn_start (the real session) via same_session_as — NOT to v1_session_birth (the presession). Resume re-binds the SAME session id (not a new one). BACKFLOW: the offline replay golden CANNOT reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar (model + tokens are sidecar-only, read live), so they are UNVERIFIABLE offline — the observation vector asserts neither model nor tokens and matches kiro-cli (agent only, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false).

text: First launch: session appears in ready (proc-<PID> presession satisfies ready before the lazy transcript materializes)
First user prompt drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready
Clean exit after the first turn: process_exited for the session (PID bound by DiscoverPID via CommandPattern + cwd)
Spec: 'Same session_id reappears (not a new session row)' — `vibe --resume <id>` reopens the SAME session_<ts>_<shortid> directory, so the SAME session id re-binds to a new PID in ready
Spec: 'ready → working on the first new turn' — the second turn drives the SAME session to working
Second turn completes back to ready on the SAME session — each lifetime's arc is independently correct
